details: An RPC-hijacking supply-chain attack disguised as an MEV-protection tool. A postinstall hook base64-decodes hxxp://165[.]22[.]200[.]211:8545, reads the victim's.env file, scans for RPC variables (RPC_URL, ETH_RPC, etc.), and overwrites them to route all Ethereum traffic through the attacker-controlled node (plain HTTP, no TLS, giving full intercept capability); it also modifies config.json. Fake benchmark output prints randomized latency numbers to appear legitimate. A preuninstall hook (keepalive.js) deliberately preserves the malicious RPC config after removal, with code comments stating the victim's bot keeps using the attacker's RPC even without the package — enabling transaction interception and front-running.
